QR Code Generator for Teachers

Free QR codes for your classroom — no sign-up, no watermark, no expiration. Generate codes for links, handouts, activities, and parent communication right in your browser. Your students' data never leaves your device.

QR codes are generated locally in your browser. Nothing is uploaded. Static codes never expire. Works with any URL your students need to reach.

How Teachers Use QR Codes in the Classroom

QR codes have moved beyond marketing gimmicks. Teachers around the world use them every day to save time, reduce paper, and make learning more interactive. Here are nine ways you can start using them tomorrow.

📚

Link to digital worksheets & handouts

Stop printing stacks of worksheets. Generate a QR code that takes students directly to a Google Doc, PDF, or online quiz. Post the code on the board and students scan to get their copy — no paper wasted, no "I lost it" excuses.

🎯

Quick-access homework and assignments

Print QR codes on assignment sheets that link to the full instructions, rubric, or example projects. Students who miss class can scan the code on a wall display or your classroom door to catch up instantly.

📖

Classroom library checkout & book reviews

Stick QR codes inside books that link to a student-written review, a summary page, or a simple checkout form. Other students can scan before borrowing to see if the book is right for them.

👩‍🏫

Differentiated instruction stations

Set up learning stations around the room with a QR code at each one. Station 1 links to a video explanation, Station 2 to a practice quiz, Station 3 to a group discussion prompt. Students rotate at their own pace.

💬

Parent communication & class updates

Include a QR code on take-home flyers and newsletters that links to the class website, calendar, or permission slip. Parents scan with their phone — no typing long URLs, no lost papers.

📹

Video tutorials & flipped classroom

Record a short explanation of a concept and host it on a simple page. Generate a QR code for the video and paste it into your slides, handouts, or even on a poster. Students watch at home and come to class ready to discuss.

🧪

Lab instructions & safety guides

In science labs, post QR codes at each station linking to the day&#39;s protocol, safety warnings, or a demonstration video. Students scan before starting — reduces questions and improves safety compliance.

🏫

Scavenger hunts & interactive activities

Design a QR code scavenger hunt around the school. Each code reveals a clue, a question, or a piece of information students need to complete a worksheet. Active learning that gets students moving.

📅

Event sign-ups & permission slips

Post a QR code on the classroom door or in the school newsletter that links to a simple sign-up form or digital permission slip. Parents scan and fill it out on the spot — no chasing down paper forms.

Why This QR Generator Works for Schools

♾️ Codes That Never Expire

Static QR codes encode the content directly into the image. There is no account, no subscription, and no server that can turn them off. Print them on flashcards, posters, or handouts — they work for years.

🔒 No Data Leaves the Device

Everything runs in the browser. Student URLs, classroom links, and any text you paste never reach a server. For schools that are cautious about data privacy, this matters.

🆓 Free for Unlimited Use

No daily cap, no premium tier for basic features, no watermark. Generate as many QR codes as you need for every class, every unit, every semester — entirely free.

Quick-Start: First QR Code in 30 Seconds

  1. Copy a link to the resource you want to share — a Google Doc, a video, a quiz, or a class website.
  2. Paste the link into the input field above and click "Generate QR Code."
  3. Download the PNG image, then paste it into your slides, worksheet, or print it as a poster for the classroom wall.
  4. Students scan with their phone camera or a QR reader app. Most modern phones scan QR codes directly from the camera app — no extra software needed.
